title: Go Workspace
content:
### Go Workspaces (Go 1.18+)

Workspaces allow working on multiple modules simultaneously.

### What is a Workspace?

A workspace is a directory with a `go.work` file that specifies a set of local modules.

### Creating a Workspace

```bash
go work init ./module1 ./module2
```

### go.work File

```
go 1.22

use (
    ./myapp
    ./mylib
    ./tools/formatter
)

replace example.com/old => ./local-fork
```

### Why Workspaces?

| Scenario | Without Workspace | With Workspace |
|----------|------------------|----------------|
| Multi-module project | Manual replace directives | Single go.work file |
| Local development | go.mod replace in each module | Centralized workspace |
| Shared tooling | Complicated setup | Simple workspace config |

### Workspace Commands

```bash
go work init      # create go.work
go work use       # add module to workspace
go work sync      # sync dependencies
go work edit      # edit go.work programmatically
```

### Workspace Example

```
project/
  go.work          -- defines workspace
  myapp/
    go.mod
    main.go
  mylib/
    go.mod
    lib.go
```

`go.work`:

```
go 1.22

use (
    ./myapp
    ./mylib
)
```

### Build with Workspace

```bash
go build ./...   # builds all modules in workspace
go test ./...    # tests all modules in workspace
```

### GOWORK Environment Variable

```bash
export GOWORK=off    # disable workspace mode
# Default: auto (detects go.work in parent directories)
```

### Workspace vs Replace

```
// Without workspace (in each go.mod):
replace example.com/mylib => ../mylib

// With workspace (single go.work):
use (
    ./myapp
    ./mylib
)
```

> **Key Point:** Workspaces are ideal for developing multiple related modules together (e.g., a library and its consumer). The `go.work` file is not committed to production repositories.

quiz.question: What file defines a Go workspace?
What command adds a module to a workspace?
